title: Select and deselect all option for checklists within updates
excerpt: We have starting making more use of the checklist feature within updates, and in some cases it would like to use these as a sort of ‘daily task’ list, that a user will tick off as they go each day, then reset it for the …

posts.author: Toploader (Jon Merchant)
posts.cookedHtml: We have starting making more use of the checklist feature within updates, and in some cases it would like to use these as a sort of ‘daily task’ list, that a user will tick off as they go each day, then reset it for the next day. As it stands, there is no way to select or deselect multiple items on a checklist, so the user has to manually untick them all at the start of each day.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Toploader - For this type of daily task reset, SubItems might work better. Have you explored using SubItems instead of a checklist? If so, I’d love to better understand why the checklist is a better fit for your use case.

posts.author: bogdan (Bogdan Chepurny)
posts.cookedHtml: Subitems don’t work as well. The formatting in Updates is much better for listing a process with checkboxes, links, and screenshots.
